title: Gate B crowd-flow relief
excerpt: Open overflow queue routing, rebalance screening, and confirm gate supervision coverage before plaza spillback grows.
body: When Gate B is backed up, dispatch the crowd-flow lead immediately, open the overflow path, rebalance screening lanes, and post an ingress advisory so queue pressure does not spread into shared circulation.
searchText: gate b crowd flow queue congestion overflow queue screening delay ingress advisory gate supervisors

title: Elevator 4 accessibility outage response
excerpt: Dispatch Facilities, hold an alternate accessible route, and communicate the restoration ETA or outage confirmation.
body: If Elevator 4 is down, facilities diagnose the fault while guest services holds the alternate accessible route and operations keeps the detour staffed until service returns or the outage is confirmed for the event window.
searchText: elevator 4 outage accessible route facilities engineer restoration eta accessibility detour

title: Section 112 wheelchair assist dispatch
excerpt: Acknowledge immediately, dispatch mobility support, confirm the accessible path, and close the handoff over radio.
body: For a wheelchair support request near Section 112, dispatch guest services with the correct mobility support, route through the accessible lower-bowl path, and confirm arrival and guest handoff before marking the assist in motion.
searchText: section 112 wheelchair access guest services mobility assist accessible path handoff radio
